page: Microsoft Azure D2as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The configuration includes 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, utilizing dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively, reflecting the 2-vCPU hardware constraint. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only and write tests consistently scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance, while database operations, static web serving, and software compilation benchmarks rank in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2as_v5 offers a low-entry hardware profile with dedicated CPU resources, making it a viable option for lightweight workloads that do not require high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-core parallel processing. It is best suited for basic general-purpose tasks, single-threaded applications, lightweight text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core with hyper-threading, 8.0 GB of memory, and no local storage, though it supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, but multi-core execution and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested systems. Single-threaded compression and text processing perform at average levels. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and modest resource footprint, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for lightweight, non-parallel workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, single-threaded applications, and small-scale development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, and 8 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance for lightweight, non-parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, and 8 GB RAM, suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU virtual machine offering strong single-core performance and balanced memory for entry-level general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz with 2 dedicated vCPUs
8.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance suitable for single-threaded tasks
Premium Storage capable configuration without local disk storage
Appropriate for lightweight web serving, text processing, and development environments
